title: Northern Irish Tart
description: A hearty, savory tart filled with the flavors of Northern Ireland, featuring a flaky crust and a rich, peppery filling. Perfect for a cold winter evening.

ingredients.name: Caramelized onions
ingredients.notes: Slow-cooked in a bit of oil until dark and sweet

ingredients.name: Peppery sausage
ingredients.notes: Use a Northern Irish-style sausage or substitute with a similar spicy sausage

instructions: Roll out the puff pastry to a thickness of about 1/8 inch. Transfer to a 9-inch tart pan with a removable bottom.
In a large skillet, cook the sausage over medium-high heat, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned and cooked through.
Add the caramelized onions and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs and heavy cream.
Sprinkle the grated cheddar cheese over the bottom of the tart crust.
Add the cooked sausage and onion mixture on top of the cheese.
Pour the egg mixture over the filling.
Bake the tart for 35-40 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is set.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 10 minutes before serving.
